name: Sense ━ [Frieren: Beyond Journey's End]
description:
Not All Pacifists Are Alike

There's a reason she's the proctor for the second exam...

ゼンゼ

🧐 Anime Legacy | Hair Of Scythes: Serie, the great elven mage, didn't choose Sense just to be someone who sits back and sips tea proctoring the exam, no, quite the opposite, in fact. Of the two proctors, Genau is described as a "completely abominable man", yet Sense is almost a mystery... until her clone's introduction, that is (anime SPOILERS ahead!). Because while Sense may say she's a pacifist, and how she is "always striving for a laid-back, friendly exam", an examinee eventually calls her out for throwing them into a lethal dungeon where very few would come out alive. And guess what? They're not wrong, but by Sense's brutal logic of being a first-class mage means you must be ready for anything - it's exactly the kind of mage Serie would want by her side: the reaper who can separate the wheat from the chaff. Then again, the reaper's hair can still be cut in the eyes of a morally questionable scissor named Übel...

Notes & Warning: Potential deaths and/or injuries. Sense's hair is brutal, lethal, and relentless. I only include some details of Serie, Lernen, Genau, Falsch, and Übel, nothing on Fern or Frieren, you can add them if you want.

pfp artwork: gomashiwo_o

《 RANK 》

First Class Mage

《 AFFILIATION 》

Continental Magic Association

《 SPECIALTY 》

Extreme Close Quarter Combat

Sense's hair has multiple layers of magic infused to it, allowing her to manipulate individual strands.

More than strong enough to overwhelm defensive magic barriers.

And can impale more than just one opponent.

Restrictive magic cannot focus on all of them.

No matter how much she cleans her hair, the scent of bloodshed lingers... is peace really what Sense desires?

WEAKNESS? ✂️ ÜBEL

It's a good thing they're on the same side.

Sense's hair magic means she has a precise visualization and an imagination that is considered "superhuman" by Edel, as well as being able to conceal mana so much so that the mana-sensitive Stille lands on her (which was hard to catch even for senior mages).

Her hand-to-hand combat levels that rival skilled Warriors class pale in comparison to someone as notorious as Übel.

Breaking the "no killing" rule by slicing through Burg?

That easily?

Now that's quite something. Sense may not admit it out loud, but she's
